chosen_evolution_point.selection_rationale: Previous iteration improved area with no delay change using match_phase.cpp. Objective hint is Area Priority and diversity is LOW, with recommended exploration including match_phase_exact.cpp. Among modules: match_phase.cpp adjusts early cost but was just used; match_drop_phase.cpp offers light cleanup but limited leverage; match_phase_exact.cpp controls cut_ref/cut_deref-based exact area trade-offs under required-time constraints, making it the strongest lever to further reduce area without violating timing. Therefore, we target match_phase_exact for a focused area improvement.

evolution_step.objective: Increase acceptance of small area reductions during exact area recovery while preserving required-time constraints
evolution_step.direction_and_strategy: Adjust the area gate threshold in the compare_map policy: change `const float area_gain_gate = 0.5f * lib_inv_area;` to `const float area_gain_gate = 0.25f * lib_inv_area;` and keep all other logic unchanged. This makes area-improving substitutions acceptable even with minor arrival degradation, still bounded by `worst_arrival <= required + epsilon`.
evolution_step.expected_impact: 0.5-1.5% area reduction; delay neutral or within epsilon (no timing violations due to required-time check)
evolution_step.constraints: Modify inside EVOLVE-BLOCK only; do not alter cut generation; maintain templates and API; keep existing epsilon comparisons and logic structure intact
evolution_step.rationale: In match_phase_exact, area improvements are currently gated to require ≥0.5× inverter area savings when arrival worsens. For Area Priority, lowering this gate to 0.25× inverter area allows more exact-area wins while the timing guard (`worst_arrival > required + epsilon` → continue) prevents degradation of required constraints. Given last iteration’s area-only gain and flat delay, this single-threshold change is a bounded, safe way to unlock additional area improvements without risking logical equivalence or timing violations.
